steps.title: No solution occurs with parallel lines
steps.content: A system of linear equations has no solution when the two lines are parallel. In slope-intercept form, parallel lines have identical slopes but different y-intercepts—they never meet.

steps.title: Check each choice for parallel lines
steps.content:
• Choice A: $y = 6x + 3$ and $y = 6x + 9$ both have slope 6, with different y-intercepts (3 and 9). Parallel—no solution.
  • Choice B: $y = 10$ has slope 0; $y = 10x + 10$ has slope 10. Different slopes—they intersect.
  • Choice C: $y = 14x + 14$ has slope 14; $y = 10x + 14$ has slope 10. Different slopes—they intersect.
  • Choice D: $x = 3$ (vertical line) and $y = 10$ (horizontal line) intersect.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
